title: Invalid response when query contains special character #0B
description:
This (invalid) query
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?g
WHERE
{
GRAPH ?g {?s a skos:Concept . }
}
ORDER BY ?g�
LIMIT 10

Where the special character is #0B (according to Notepad++ - Github reencodes it) causes Virtuoso SPARQL endpoint to do "invalid response" according to Chrome.
This happens with any query that contains this character. It got to the query when copy&pasting from MS PowerPoint (attached), where it can be created by pressing shift+enter for a newline in the same paragraph.
sparql-bug.pptx
I would expect a standard error message.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Concept A');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Concept B\x0B');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE '%\x0B%';
UPDATE test_table SET name = NULL WHERE id = 1;
key_bug_features: Query fails with special character #0B in SPARQL
